text: If you're chasing sun before things get hazier, get after it early: today's the pick, mostly sunny with a high near 87 and just a slight chance of a stray shower between 3 and 5. Air quality has slipped to moderate with wildfire smoke drifting in, so anyone sensitive should ease up on the hard outdoor efforts this afternoon, and that smoke thickens up patchy overnight before a front pushes it south. The lake's in good shape today, west wind 5 to 15 knots building to 15 to 20 out of the northwest by afternoon, waves a foot or less, water sitting at a pleasant 76. Thursday brings more uncertainty: NWS has a 50% chance of afternoon showers and storms, and the models are all over the map on it, with the European model soaking Thursday and the American and Canadian guidance much drier, so I'd call it a coin flip and keep the rain plan handy. Bottom line: get your lake time in today before 3, and treat Thursday afternoon as iffy for anything outdoors.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-15T11:00:00+00:00): Mostly Clear, 75F feels 76F, humidity 74%, wind W 8 mph
NWS FORECAST:
  Today: A slight chance of rain showers between 3pm and 5pm. Mostly sunny, with a high near 87. Northwest wind 5 to 14 mph, with gusts as high as 29 mph. Chance of precipitation is 20%. New rainfall amounts between a tenth and quarter of an inch possible.
  Tonight: Patchy smoke between midnight and 4am. Mostly clear, with a low around 66. West wind 3 to 12 mph, with gusts as high as 26 mph. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.
  Thursday: A chance of showers and thunderstorms after 2pm. Mostly sunny, with a high near 84. West wind 5 to 16 mph, with gusts as high as 26 mph. Chance of precipitation is 50%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.
  Thursday Night: Partly cloudy, with a low around 59. Northwest wind 6 to 13 mph, with gusts as high as 23 mph. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-15T09:21:00+00:00):
  1. Storms moving away today. A few showers will linger.
  2. Smoky conditions through Thursday until a reinforcing frontal boundary brings rain and pushes it south.
  3. Seasonable temperatures and showers likely this weekend with possible thunderstorms, then another round of showers and potential storms early next week.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 514 AM EDT Wednesday... The Severe Thunderstorm Watch has ended. A few showers will linger early. Smoke has been added to parts of northern New York and Vermont Wednesday evening into Thursday ahead of a cold front.
LAKE (broad waters):
  Today: West winds 5 to 15 knots, becoming northwest 15 to 20 knots. Partly sunny. Waves 1 foot or less.
  Tonight: Northwest winds 5 to 15 knots, becoming west. Clear. Patchy smoke overnight. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Thursday: West winds 5 to 15 knots, becoming northwest 15 to 20 knots. Sunny in the morning, then becoming partly sunny. A chance of showers and thunderstorms in the afternoon. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Thursday Night: Northwest winds 5 to 15 knots. Partly cloudy in the evening, then clearing. Waves 1 foot or less.
LAKE GAGE: water 76F, level 96.32 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-15: highs [GFS 84, Euro 80, ICON 79] spread 5F; precip chance [GFS 68%, Euro 97%, ICON 35%]
  2026-07-16: highs [GFS 82, Euro 82, ICON 79] spread 3F; precip chance [GFS 45%, Euro 61%, ICON 8%]
  2026-07-17: highs [GFS 74, Euro 75, ICON 74] spread 1F; precip chance [GFS 2%, Euro 0%, ICON 2%]
AIR: AQI 94 Moderate (PM2.5) — On Wednesday, July 15th, expect moderate air quality in Vermont, as smoke from Midwest and Canadian wildfires impacts the region.
SUN: rise 2026-07-15T05:22-04:00, set 2026-07-15T20:34-04:00, UV max 6.0
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high 83, low 65: Morning clouds followed by afternoon sun. Highs in the low 80s and lows in the mid 60s.
  high 79, low 59: Times of sun and clouds. Highs in the upper 70s and lows in the upper 50s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: It’s likely that temperatures will stay in the upper 70s for Friday, but hopefully with clearer skies. Then we’ll stay cooler into the weekend with highs in the 70s and a few chances for rain. There should still be enough dry time to enjoy the weekend, we’ll have more details on the weekend forecast later this week.
NBC5 FIRST WARNING (2026-07-14T22:57:00Z): Overnight severe storms, smoke lingers with dropping humidity Wednesday — Large hail, damaging wind for a few towns; highs fall back to the 80s the rest of the week
